name: Broccoli
description:
I know where your house lives.

The flag lives in /flag not ./flag, not /flag.txt

name: Browser pwn
description:
Discord wars are getting crazier. Even your l33t hacker friends are getting their IPs d0xed and their minecraft servers griefed.
Its time for revenge and you need to up your game: You need to find some real dangerous browser exploits to make the difference in this fight.

At least your target was friendly enough to have a public repo with their nix config so you can reproduce their environment, I heard thats really useful for hardcore binary exploitation!1!elf

name: Check This Out
description:
In the spirit of open source I publish my code, though I wish there existed
a better version control than project-latest-final-final-2.tar.zst.

I love checking out what other people do with my code, but in this cruel
world you need to take precautions.

Therefore, I only check out the good code I have written myself.


Looking forward to your submissions!

name: CustomDice
description:
Need some tools for your local board game rounds? 
CustomDice has got you covered. There are even 
some extra special tools for admins.

Rumor has it the admin tools are free for everyone 
who can solve the login riddle.

name: Free Parking Network
description:
Do you like to pay for hosting? No. I have the perfect solution for you!

Free Parking Network is a globally distributed, free hosting service that allows you to host your websites without any cost. It is designed to be easy to use and provides a simple interface for deploying your websites now.

LEGAL: After a thorough review from our legal team your websites will be served to the world. We reserve the right to remove your website at any time without notice. We are not responsible for any content that you host on our platform. By using Free Parking Network, you agree to our terms and conditions.

name: Free Parking Network v2
description:
After multiple take down requests from law enforcement, we made our site even more secure. Now an admin needs to review all uploaded websites before they go live.

But remember, we reserve the right to remove your website at any time without notice. We are not responsible for any content that you host on our platform. By using Free Parking Network, you agree to our terms and conditions.

name: Free-Flagging
description: "You reached Free Parking" - sadly we ran out of money, but here have a free flag instead.

name: Git Gud
description:
While writing another challenge, I accidentally beheaded my editor :(

Can you ge~~i~~t gud and put it's head back on? Or just get the flag...

name: hinting
description: C'mon take a hint, you want to play that.

name: Honeypot
description:
I've just bought this property in a very priviledged part of the system.

But there seem to be(e) awfully many bees around. I just hope I can find a way
out of this *thing* the developer has constructed here before I get stung...

*First blood price*: For this challenge there is a separate first blood prize
in form of a topical T-Shirt! Sadly we have to limit this prize to either
on-site collection at GPN or shipping within Germany.

name: Image Contest
description:
You have technicians here, making noise, they are not artists because no one has submitted any art yet.
Which is exactly what you need to do: submit a banger image and win the flag.
Sadly, the image contest doesn't end until the end of the CTF, so you will need to expedite the process.

name: Intro to web - Part 1
description: 5 vulns, 5 stages - can you find them all?

name: Intro to web - Part 2
description: 5 vulns, 5 stages - can you find them all?

name: Intro to web - Part 3
description: 5 vulns, 5 stages - can you find them all?

name: Intro to web - Part 4
description: 5 vulns, 5 stages - can you find them all?

name: Intro to web - Part 5
description: 5 vulns, 5 stages - can you find them all?

name: jail
description:
GO DIRECTLY TO JAIL DO NOT PASS GO, DO NOT COLLECT $200.

**Important**: Spawn instance and enter team token as normal. Connect with `stty raw -echo; ncat --ssl YOUR_INSTANCE_NAME.gpn23.ctf.kitctf.de 443` in an interactive **Bash** shell. Fish and Zsh are known to not work.

name: JShellCode
description:
Oh, wie schön ist Panama! Grab yourself a friend
(and a little wooden tiger duck) and take a trip
through an island in Indonesia and middle America.
And maybe, once you've reached your destination,
you'll realize it was most beautiful in x86-Land,
right where you started.

Announcement: The image base of the handout Dockerfile is incorrect,
the correct base is `eclipse-temurin:23-jdk@sha256:83177670e43c17769a5402a60601545318e45e63adf634fb5022340e516cded4`.  
Sorry about any time you wasted on that :(

name: MiniDSP
description:
I found this compiler, I compiled this code, now I am confused.

I hope you are as well.

Note that the code might be broken.

name: NASA
description: Why even bother writing secure code when you can just enable sanitizers?

name: no-nc
description: I've only heard bad things about nc so I banned it.

name: Note Editor
description: The web guys always have these note apps, why not use this terminal based one instead.

name: PAINting Dice
description: Be part of a dedicated dice painting group. If you are part of the secret club I may tell you my favorite color.

name: Paranoid
description:
I think someone messed with my hardware.

I swear that randomness is worse than on PlayStation 3.

I cannot trust it.

I only use what I understand and can build in software.

name: Pro(v|b)ably secure
description:
We have probable intel that our target tried to prove their flag?!

As you can see in the leaked sources, the admin user
placed a flag into a model. It is vital that you get access to their
account and recover the flag.

We are also very excited to tell you that our agents are certain that
you don't have to touch anything related to the prover itself, just
recover the flag and get out of this hell as quickly as possible!

name: Real Christmas
description:
Please don't ask how this relates to Christmas, you wouldn't get it.

It's inspired by some real code I found in a project.

name: Real Christmas V2
description:
Please don't ask how this relates to Christmas, you wouldn't get it.

It's inspired by some real code I found in a project.

Enjoy this slightly harder version.

name: restricted oracle
description:
Everbody can exploit oracles.  
Do you have what it takes to exploit a restricted oracle?  
If you do you might find a flag between all the german words I placed in there.

name: Sanity Check
description: I've read the rules, have you?

name: Secure by Default
description:
Just look at how amazing and fast the new way of writing web applications in PHP with Laravel has become!

For sure, it is secure by default, right?

Hint:

The code was generated using `laravel new i-love-php --pest --breeze --dark --stack=livewire -q`  along time ago

You might want to run `composer audit`

name: Smile at me
description:
Be careful, others might be able to find out your most sacred secrets! (Flag only consists of emojis surrounded by 'GPNCTF{...}')

The remote instance is not deployed via Docker-compose but plain Docker, resulting in the bot URL to be 'localhost:3000' instead of 'bot_service:3000' and the challenge server being 'localhost:9222' instead of 'challenge_service:9222'.

name: Terminator 2
description:
After last year's Terminator 01 challenge, [some
people](https://platypwnies.de/writeups/2024/gpn/pwn/terminator-1/)
correctly deduced that somehow terminator returned… A mystery for the ages!

But, *this* time the stacks are actually stacked against you! I fear
you will have to find another way... The Terminator is a bit trigger happy,
so don't fret too much if your exploit is slightly unstable.

name: TES
description:
I wanted to setup encrypted communication for my smart toaster, but it was just too slow. 

I removed some parts that were not required to get it running.

Please don't hack my toaster, I really tried my best.

name: The old way
description:
Back then, one had to put a lot of manual work in to get
efficient code. Nowadays, they say the compiler just does
it. But I don't trust them...

name: variants
description:
A true cosmopolitan should speak many languages. And I
do... but everywhere I go, people seem to understand
different things...

Could you help me find a consensus they can all agree
on? Someone might even give you a flag for it.

name: WebGL-bird
description:
I found this nice new browser called Ladybird.
It would be a shame if one would find a 0day in it and exploit it.

NOTE: The patches are irrelevant to the challenge, they are just there to make WebGL "work" on Linux.
The use of the old ladybird version is also irrelevant.

name: Yavascript Blog
description: YAML is the best javascript object notation. So I made a blog hoster with YAML + JS!

name: Yavascript Blog: CSP Edition
description:
YAML is the best javascript object notation. So I made a blog hoster with YAML + JS!

The haters keep saying that YAML is "so unsafe" and "a bad language" so I added a CSP to my Node.js app to make it super secure!

NEW HINT:
- the YAML spec is not real and can safely be ignored
- javascript arrays and iterators are just spicy objects
- prototypes can be a lot of types
- just one pollution would be boring
- before trying things that will never work: the "CSP" is secure, unless you have a v8 zeroday you cannot use string evaluation
